task_description:
# Wrap `react-h5-audio-player` as a Reflex NoSSRComponent with Playlist State

## Background
Reflex compiles Python UI into a Next.js / React frontend. Many useful UI libraries are only available on npm. The [`react-h5-audio-player`](https://github.com/lhz516/react-h5-audio-player) package ships a `<AudioPlayer />` React component that touches `window.Audio`, so it must be wrapped as a Reflex `NoSSRComponent` (see [Wrapping React Overview](https://reflex.dev/docs/wrapping-react/overview/)). Your job is to wrap it, expose two props and three event triggers, and drive it from a small playlist state machine.

## Requirements
- Build a Reflex app named `audio_app` at `/home/user/audio_app` using `uv` per the rules in the project plan (use `uv init`, `uv add reflex`, `uv run reflex init --template blank`).
- Author a Python wrapper for `react-h5-audio-player` as a `NoSSRComponent`.
- The wrapper must expose **exactly** these two props as Reflex Vars: `src: rx.Var[str]` and `autoplay: rx.Var[bool]`. The Python attribute names must literally be `src` and `autoplay`; you are responsible for mapping them onto the underlying React props (the React component uses `src` and `autoPlay`).
- The wrapper must declare three event triggers â€” `on_play`, `on_pause`, `on_ended` â€” each defined via `rx.EventHandler[...]` with a serializer lambda that produces **zero** positional arguments (i.e. the event handler signature on the Python side takes no extra args).
- Build a single page at route `/` that displays:
  - The wrapped audio player driven by the current playlist track.
  - A list of exactly 3 selectable track entries; clicking a track sets that track as the current one.
- Implement a `State` class (subclass of `rx.State`) with:
  - A backend list of exactly 3 tracks (each with a `title` and a `src` URL â€” use local placeholder paths such as `/tracks/track1.mp3`, etc.; do **not** read any external service).
  - An `int` Base Var named `current_index` representing the active track.
  - An event handler that updates `current_index` when a track is clicked.
  - An event handler wired to the wrapper's `on_ended` trigger that advances `current_index` by one and **loops back to 0** after the last track.
- The page must bind the wrapper's `src` Var to the current track's URL so that switching the index swaps the audio source.

## Implementation Hints
- Read the React component's prop and event names from the upstream README before wiring up the wrapper.
- `NoSSRComponent` lives in `reflex.components.component`.
- `react-h5-audio-player` uses a **default** export; consider the `is_default` class attribute.
- Reflex auto-converts snake_case Python prop names to camelCase on the React side, but a single word like `autoplay` will not magically become `autoPlay`. Look into prop renaming mechanisms (e.g. `_rename_props`) when the names diverge.
- The library ships a separate CSS file (`react-h5-audio-player/lib/styles.css`). You will need to inject it into the compiled output (e.g. via `add_imports` / `_get_custom_code` / `add_style` â€” pick whichever the docs you read recommend).
- For event triggers that should serialize zero arguments, the lambda used in `rx.EventHandler[...]` takes no parameters and returns `[]`.
- All Python dependencies must be managed with `uv`. After installing, generate `requirements.txt` (e.g. `uv pip freeze > requirements.txt`) at the project root so the project remains Reflex-Cloud-deployable per `plan.md`.
- **Background servers MUST be killed before finishing the task.** Do not leave `reflex run`, `reflex export`, or any other Reflex process listening on ports 3000 or 8000. The verifier will start its own short-lived processes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: `/home/user/audio_app`
- Start command: `uv run reflex run --loglevel debug`
- Port: frontend on `3000`, backend on `8000`
- A file `requirements.txt` exists at the project root listing `reflex` (any version `>=0.6.6`).
- A Python module inside the project defines a class that subclasses `reflex.components.component.NoSSRComponent` (directly or indirectly) and:
  - Sets `library` to a string whose first token (before any `@version` suffix) is exactly `react-h5-audio-player`.
  - Sets a `tag` attribute (any non-empty string).
  - Sets `is_default = True`.
  - Declares a class attribute `src` typed as `rx.Var[str]`.
  - Declares a class attribute `autoplay` typed as `rx.Var[bool]`.
  - Declares three event triggers `on_play`, `on_pause`, `on_ended`, each annotated as `rx.EventHandler[...]` with a serializer lambda that takes no arguments and returns an empty list (`[]`).
- A `State` class (subclass of `rx.State`) exposes:
  - A list-typed Base Var holding exactly 3 track entries.
  - An `int` Base Var named `current_index` defaulting to `0`.
  - An event handler that, given an integer index, sets `current_index` to that integer.
  - An event handler bound to the wrapper's `on_ended` trigger that advances `current_index` to `(current_index + 1) % 3`.
- Building the frontend with `uv run reflex export --frontend-only --no-zip` succeeds, and the resulting compiled assets reference the string `react-h5-audio-player` (i.e. the wrapper survived bundling).
- After the task is complete, no Reflex process is running on ports 3000 or 8000.

## Notes
- Do not set any external environment variables for the playlist; the 3 placeholder URLs are part of the source code.
- Do not deploy to Reflex Cloud; only local export is required.
task_integrations:
- Reflex (target framework)
- npm package `react-h5-audio-player` (bundled at compile time; no runtime third-party service)
truth:
# Verification Plan

## Setup
1. Ensure no Reflex processes are listening on ports 3000 or 8000 before running checks (kill any leftover `reflex run` / `next` / `uvicorn` from the agent if found, then proceed).
2. Inside `/home/user/audio_app`, all verification commands run via `uv run <cmd>` so that the project's virtualenv (with `reflex` installed) is used. The verifier itself uses system `python3` and only shells out to `uv` for Reflex-specific work.
3. If a previous run left `/home/user/audio_app/.web` around, it is fine to keep it; the verifier will re-export.

## Verification Steps

1. **Project layout**
   - Check that `/home/user/audio_app/rxconfig.py` exists.
   - Check that `/home/user/audio_app/requirements.txt` exists and contains a line starting with `reflex` (case-insensitive).
   - Confirm no process is currently bound to TCP ports 3000 or 8000 (e.g. via `ss -lntp` / `lsof -i`).

2. **Wrapper static analysis** (parse the project's Python source files under `/home/user/audio_app` with `ast`, recursively, ignoring `.web/` and `.venv/`):
   - Find at least one `ClassDef` whose bases include a name ending in `NoSSRComponent` (e.g. `NoSSRComponent`, `rx.NoSSRComponent`, or via `from reflex.components.component import NoSSRComponent`).
   - In that class body, assert:
     - An assignment `library = <str literal>` whose value, when split on `@`, has first token `react-h5-audio-player`.
     - An assignment `tag = <non-empty str literal>`.
     - An assignment `is_default = True`.
     - An `AnnAssign` for `src` whose annotation source contains `rx.Var[str]` or `Var[str]`.
     - An `AnnAssign` for `autoplay` whose annotation source contains `rx.Var[bool]` or `Var[bool]`.
     - Three `AnnAssign` nodes for `on_play`, `on_pause`, `on_ended`, each whose annotation source mentions `EventHandler` and whose subscript contains a `Lambda` node taking **zero arguments** and returning an empty `List` (`[]`).

3. **State static analysis** (same AST walk):
   - Find at least one `ClassDef` whose bases include `rx.State` (or any base whose name ends with `State` and resolves to a Reflex state).
   - Assert it defines a Base Var `current_index` annotated as `int` with default `0`.
   - Assert it defines a list-typed Base Var (any name) whose default value is a list literal of exactly 3 elements.
   - Assert there is at least one `FunctionDef` whose body contains an assignment to `self.current_index` from an `int` parameter (or via subscripting).
   - Assert there is at least one `FunctionDef` whose body assigns `self.current_index` to an expression containing the modulo operator (`%`) with the literal `3` (the looping advance handler).

4. **State behaviour smoke test via `uv run python`** â€” run a short Python snippet inside `/home/user/audio_app` using `uv run python -c '...'`:
   - Import the user's app module(s) and locate the `State` subclass and the playlist list var.
   - Construct an instance directly (`StateCls()`), call the click handler with index `2`, and assert `current_index == 2`.
   - From that state, call the `on_ended` handler three times and assert the sequence of `current_index` values goes `2 -> 0 -> 1 -> 2` (i.e. (index+1) % 3 wraparound).
   - The `uv run python -c '...'` invocation must exit with code 0; any stderr from this script is logged but the exit code is what counts.

5. **Frontend build references the npm package**
   - Run `uv run reflex export --frontend-only --no-zip` inside `/home/user/audio_app` with a 5-minute timeout. The command must exit with code 0.
   - After the export, recursively search the produced `.web/_static/` (or whichever directory `reflex export` emits) for the literal string `react-h5-audio-player` in any text file (`*.js`, `*.html`, `*.css`, `*.json`). The string must appear at least once. If `.web/_static/` is empty, also search `.web/.next/` as a fallback.

6. **Teardown check**
   - After the export step, confirm again that no process is bound to TCP ports 3000 or 8000. The agent is expected to leave the environment clean.
